reference.title: Substitution variables in property group pages

titlealts.navtitle: Substitution variables in property group pages
titlealts.searchtitle: Substitution variables in property group pages

reference.shortdesc: You can use substitution variables in property group pages to increase their flexibility and portability.

refbody.section:
The property group pages support the following substitution variables:
Variable Description Where it is allowed
<HLQ> The current high-level qualifier.
This variable gets its value from two different places, depending on where you are using it in the product.
In the z/OS Projects view, <HLQ> comes from the HLQ property that is set on the subproject. The values available for <HLQ> in the subproject come from the filters that you set in the Remote Systems view. In a subproject, <HLQ> resolves to the value that is set on the subproject.
In the Remote Systems view, the <HLQ> is the leftmost qualifier of the fully qualified data set member that you selected for the action.
Any field that takes a data set name.
<JOBNAME> A generated job name for batch jobs. JCL job card on the JCL Job Card and Data Set page.
<MEM> The current data set member that you selected and upon which the action is run. If you are building a subproject, it is the current member that is processed in the course of the subproject build. Any field that takes a member name.
<USERID> The user ID that you used to connect to the remote system.
